question_title: How many ICJ judgments, advisory opinions, and orders dated from March 5, 2026 through April 30, 2026 inclusive will appear on the ICJ Decisions page before April 30, 2026?
summary:
The ICJ Decisions page historically lists ~22-27 entries per year (2023: 26, 2024: 27, 2025: ~21), including judgments, advisory opinions, and procedural orders (time-limit fixings, provisional measures, etc.). This averages ~2.06 decisions/month or ~0.068/day.

For the March 5 - April 30, 2026 window (57 days), I've confirmed 1 entry: the March 19, 2026 Judgment granting Guatemala permission to intervene in Belize v. Honduras. No other entries appear for March 5-18.

Using a Poisson model (λ=2.77 for the remaining 41 days, conditioned on 1 known entry), the expected total is ~3.8 entries with median of 4. The distribution is slightly right-skewed with P10=2, P90=6.

Key upcoming activity: multiple Article 63 intervention declarations in S. Africa v. Israel may generate admissibility decisions; various cases in written proceedings could produce time-limit fixing orders. The Gambia v. Myanmar merits judgment is unlikely before April 30 (hearings ended only Jan 29, 2026).

factors.description: Historical base rate: ICJ decisions page averages ~24.7 entries/year (2023: 26, 2024: 27, 2025: ~21), giving λ≈3.85 for 57-day window. Poisson model conditioned on 1 known entry yields median of 4.
One entry already confirmed in window: March 19, 2026 Judgment on Guatemala's intervention application in Belize v. Honduras case. This sets a hard floor of 1.
Multiple Article 63 intervention declarations filed in S. Africa v. Israel (March 3, 11, 12 by 7+ countries). Court may issue admissibility decision(s) that would appear as entries.
ICJ decisions cluster: batches of 2-3 procedural orders often issued same day (Feb 25: 3 orders, Dec 17: 2). This increases variance beyond Poisson.
Gambia v. Myanmar merits judgment unlikely by April 30 - hearings concluded Jan 29, 2026, and major judgments typically take 6+ months after hearings.
2025 annual count (~21) notably lower than 2023-2024 (~26-27), suggesting recent period may have lower decision rate, pulling distribution slightly left.

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

The ICJ Decisions page will likely show **3-5 entries** dated March 5 through April 30, 2026, with a **median of 4**.

**Key findings:**
- **1 confirmed entry**: March 19, 2026 Judgment (Guatemala intervention in Belize v. Honduras)
- **Historical base rate**: ~2 decisions/month on the ICJ decisions page (including procedural orders)
- **Poisson model** with λ≈3.85 for the 57-day window, conditioned on 1 known entry

**Distribution:**
| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 2 |
| P25 | 3 |
| P50 (median) | 4 |
| P75 | 5 |
| P90 | 6 |
| P95 | 7 |
sources_consulted: [List of International Court of Justice cases - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_International_Court_of_Justice_cases)

[(Metaculus) How many ICJ judgments, advisory opinions, and orders dated from March 5, 2026 through April 30, 2026 inclusive will appear on the ICJ Decisions page before April 30, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42648)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the numeric value of the number of entries dated March 5, 2026 through April 30, 2026 inclusive that appear on the ICJ “Judgments, Advisory Opinions and Orders” page at [https://www.icj-cij.org/decisions](https://www.icj-cij.org/decisions) before April 30, 2026.
fine_print:
Count only entries that appear on the ICJ Decisions page itself. Count judgments, advisory opinions, and orders, and count each entry once even if it is available in more than one language. Ignore press releases, hearing schedules, and other case updates that do not appear as entries on the Decisions page.

If the ICJ Decisions page contains duplicate postings of the same decision (including entries in different languages or obvious posting errors), they will count as a single qualifying entry only.
